BayesianQDM 0.1.0

    • Single Continuous Endpoint
    • Two Binary Endpoints
    • Two Continuous Endpoints
  • Changelog

Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for Two Continuous Endpoints

Source: R/pbayesdecisionprob2cont.R
pbayesdecisionprob2cont.Rd

Computes the operating characteristics (Go, NoGo, Gray, and optionally Miss probabilities) for a two-continuous-endpoint Bayesian Go/NoGo decision framework by Monte Carlo simulation over treatment scenarios.

Usage

pbayesdecisionprob2cont(
  nsim,
  prob,
  design,
  prior,
  GoRegions,
  NoGoRegions,
  gamma1,
  gamma2,
  theta.TV1 = NULL,
  theta.MAV1 = NULL,
  theta.TV2 = NULL,
  theta.MAV2 = NULL,
  theta.NULL1 = NULL,
  theta.NULL2 = NULL,
  n1,
  n2 = NULL,
  m1 = NULL,
  m2 = NULL,
  mu1,
  Sigma1,
  mu2,
  Sigma2,
  kappa01 = NULL,
  nu01 = NULL,
  mu01 = NULL,
  Lambda01 = NULL,
  kappa02 = NULL,
  nu02 = NULL,
  mu02 = NULL,
  Lambda02 = NULL,
  r = NULL,
  ne1 = NULL,
  ne2 = NULL,
  alpha01e = NULL,
  alpha02e = NULL,
  ybar_e1 = NULL,
  ybar_e2 = NULL,
  Se1 = NULL,
  Se2 = NULL,
  nMC = NULL,
  method = "MC",
  error_if_Miss = TRUE,
  Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE,
  seed
)

Arguments

nsim

A positive integer. Number of simulated datasets per scenario.

prob

Character scalar: 'posterior' or 'predictive'.

design

Character scalar: 'controlled', 'uncontrolled', or 'external'.

prior

Character scalar: 'vague' or 'N-Inv-Wishart'.

GoRegions

Integer vector. Region indices (1–9 for posterior, 1–4 for predictive) that trigger a Go decision.

NoGoRegions

Integer vector. Region indices that trigger a NoGo decision. Must be disjoint from GoRegions.

gamma1

Numeric scalar in (0, 1). Go threshold: Go if \(P(\mathrm{GoRegions}) \ge \gamma_1\).

gamma2

Numeric scalar in (0, 1). NoGo threshold: NoGo if \(P(\mathrm{NoGoRegions}) \ge \gamma_2\).

theta.TV1

Numeric scalar or NULL. Target value for Endpoint 1 (required when prob = 'posterior').

theta.MAV1

Numeric scalar or NULL. Minimum acceptable value for Endpoint 1 (required when prob = 'posterior').

theta.TV2

Numeric scalar or NULL. Target value for Endpoint 2 (required when prob = 'posterior').

theta.MAV2

Numeric scalar or NULL. Minimum acceptable value for Endpoint 2 (required when prob = 'posterior').

theta.NULL1

Numeric scalar or NULL. Null value for Endpoint 1 (required when prob = 'predictive').

theta.NULL2

Numeric scalar or NULL. Null value for Endpoint 2 (required when prob = 'predictive').

n1

Positive integer. Treatment arm sample size.

n2

Positive integer or NULL. Control arm sample size (required when design is 'controlled' or 'external').

m1

Positive integer or NULL. Future treatment arm size (required when prob = 'predictive').

m2

Positive integer or NULL. Future control arm size (required when prob = 'predictive').

mu1

Numeric matrix with 2 columns. Each row gives the true treatment mean vector for one scenario. A length-2 vector is coerced to a 1-row matrix.

Sigma1

A 2x2 positive-definite matrix. True treatment covariance.

mu2

Numeric matrix with 2 columns or a length-2 vector. True control (or hypothetical control) mean vector(s).

Sigma2

A 2x2 positive-definite matrix. True control covariance.

kappa01

Positive numeric or NULL. NIW prior pseudo sample size for treatment (required when prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart').

nu01

Positive numeric or NULL. NIW prior degrees of freedom for treatment (required when prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart').

mu01

Length-2 numeric vector or NULL. NIW prior mean for treatment (required when prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart').

Lambda01

A 2x2 numeric matrix or NULL. NIW prior scale matrix for treatment (required when prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart').

kappa02

Positive numeric or NULL. NIW prior pseudo sample size for control (required when design is 'controlled' or 'external' and prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart').

nu02

Positive numeric or NULL. NIW prior degrees of freedom for control.

mu02

Length-2 numeric vector or NULL. NIW prior mean for control, or the hypothetical control mean when design = 'uncontrolled'.

Lambda02

A 2x2 numeric matrix or NULL. NIW prior scale matrix for control.

r

Positive numeric or NULL. Variance scaling ratio for the hypothetical control arm (required when design = 'uncontrolled').

ne1

Positive integer or NULL. External treatment sample size (used when design = 'external').

ne2

Positive integer or NULL. External control sample size (used when design = 'external').

alpha01e

Numeric scalar in (0, 1]. Power prior weight for external treatment data.

alpha02e

Numeric scalar in (0, 1]. Power prior weight for external control data.

ybar_e1

Length-2 numeric vector. External treatment sample mean.

ybar_e2

Length-2 numeric vector. External control sample mean.

Se1

A 2x2 numeric matrix. External treatment sum-of-squares matrix.

Se2

A 2x2 numeric matrix. External control sum-of-squares matrix.

nMC

Positive integer or NULL. Number of Monte Carlo draws passed to pbayespostpred2cont to compute region probabilities for each simulated dataset. Required when method = 'MC'; may be set to NULL when method = 'MM' (the MM method uses analytical mvtnorm::pmvt and does not require MC draws). Default NULL.

method

Character scalar: 'MC' or 'MM' (Moment-Matching via mvtnorm::pmvt). Default 'MC'.

error_if_Miss

Logical scalar. If TRUE (default), the function stops when any scenario yields a positive Miss probability.

Gray_inc_Miss

Logical scalar. If TRUE, Miss probability is folded into Gray. If FALSE (default), Miss is reported separately. Active only when error_if_Miss = FALSE.

seed

A numeric value for reproducible random number generation.

Value

A data.frame with one row per scenario and columns mu1_ep1, mu1_ep2 (and mu2_ep1, mu2_ep2 for controlled/external), Go, Gray, NoGo, and optionally Miss. The object has S3 class c("pbayesdecisionprob2cont", "data.frame") with all input parameters attached as attributes.

Details

The function follows the same structure as pbayesdecisionprob1cont:

  1. For each scenario \(s\), nsim datasets are simulated by generating treatment (and control) observations from \(N_2(\mu_k^{(s)}, \Sigma_k)\). To minimise overhead, raw standardised residuals are generated once (scenario- invariant) and shifted by the scenario mean.

  2. All nsim simulated sufficient statistics \((\bar{y}_{1,i}, S_{1,i})\) (and \((\bar{y}_{2,i}, S_{2,i})\) for controlled/external designs) are passed to pbayespostpred2cont in a single vectorised call, returning an \(nsim \times n_{\rm regions}\) matrix of region probabilities.

  3. Go/NoGo/Miss probabilities are obtained as the column means of indicator matrices derived from the region probability matrix.

Examples

# Example 1: Controlled design, posterior probability, vague prior
Sigma <- matrix(c(4.0, 0.8, 0.8, 1.0), 2, 2)
pbayesdecisionprob2cont(
  nsim = 100L, prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled',
  prior = 'vague',
  GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,
  gamma1 = 0.8, gamma2 = 0.8,
  theta.TV1 = 1.5, theta.MAV1 = 0.5,
  theta.TV2 = 1.0, theta.MAV2 = 0.3,
  theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,
  n1 = 20L, n2 = 20L, m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,
  mu1 = rbind(c(1.0, 0.5), c(2.5, 1.5), c(4.0, 2.5)),
  Sigma1 = Sigma,
  mu2 = rbind(c(0.0, 0.0), c(0.0, 0.0), c(0.0, 0.0)),
  Sigma2 = Sigma,
  kappa01 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, mu01 = NULL, Lambda01 = NULL,
  kappa02 = NULL, nu02 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, Lambda02 = NULL,
  r = NULL,
  ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01e = NULL, alpha02e = NULL,
  ybar_e1 = NULL, ybar_e2 = NULL, Se1 = NULL, Se2 = NULL,
  nMC = 500L, method = 'MC',
  error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE, seed = 1L
)
#> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Two Continuous Endpoints)
#> ----------------------------------------------------------------- 
#>   Probability type : posterior
#>   Design           : controlled
#>   Prior            : vague
#>   Simulations      : nsim = 100, nMC = 500
#>   Method           : MC
#>   Seed             : 1
#>   Threshold(s)     : TV1 = 1.5, MAV1 = 0.5, TV2 = 1, MAV2 = 0.3
#>   Go  threshold    : gamma1 = 0.8
#>   NoGo threshold   : gamma2 = 0.8
#>   Go  regions      : {1}
#>   NoGo regions     : {9}
#>   Sample size      : n1 = 20, n2 = 20
#>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE
#> ----------------------------------------------------------------- 
#>  mu1_ep1 mu1_ep2 mu2_ep1 mu2_ep2     Go   Gray   NoGo
#>      1.0     0.5       0       0 0.0100 0.9800 0.0100
#>      2.5     1.5       0       0 0.5500 0.4500 0.0000
#>      4.0     2.5       0       0 1.0000 0.0000 0.0000
#> ----------------------------------------------------------------- 

# Example 2: Controlled design, posterior probability, NIW prior
Sigma <- matrix(c(4.0, 0.8, 0.8, 1.0), 2, 2)
L0    <- matrix(c(8.0, 0.0, 0.0, 2.0), 2, 2)
pbayesdecisionprob2cont(
  nsim = 100L, prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled',
  prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart',
  GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,
  gamma1 = 0.8, gamma2 = 0.8,
  theta.TV1 = 1.5, theta.MAV1 = 0.5,
  theta.TV2 = 1.0, theta.MAV2 = 0.3,
  theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,
  n1 = 20L, n2 = 20L, m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,
  mu1 = rbind(c(1.0, 0.5), c(2.5, 1.5), c(4.0, 2.5)),
  Sigma1 = Sigma,
  mu2 = rbind(c(0.0, 0.0), c(0.0, 0.0), c(0.0, 0.0)),
  Sigma2 = Sigma,
  kappa01 = 2.0, nu01 = 5.0, mu01 = c(2.0, 1.0), Lambda01 = L0,
  kappa02 = 2.0, nu02 = 5.0, mu02 = c(0.0, 0.0), Lambda02 = L0,
  r = NULL,
  ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01e = NULL, alpha02e = NULL,
  ybar_e1 = NULL, ybar_e2 = NULL, Se1 = NULL, Se2 = NULL,
  nMC = 500L, method = 'MC',
  error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE, seed = 2L
)
#> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Two Continuous Endpoints)
#> ----------------------------------------------------------------- 
#>   Probability type : posterior
#>   Design           : controlled
#>   Prior            : N-Inv-Wishart
#>   Simulations      : nsim = 100, nMC = 500
#>   Method           : MC
#>   Seed             : 2
#>   Threshold(s)     : TV1 = 1.5, MAV1 = 0.5, TV2 = 1, MAV2 = 0.3
#>   Go  threshold    : gamma1 = 0.8
#>   NoGo threshold   : gamma2 = 0.8
#>   Go  regions      : {1}
#>   NoGo regions     : {9}
#>   Sample size      : n1 = 20, n2 = 20
#>   Prior Grp1 (NIW) : kappa01 = 2, nu01 = 5
#>                      mu01 = (2, 1), Lambda01 = [8, 0, 0, 2]
#>   Prior Grp2 (NIW) : kappa02 = 2, nu02 = 5
#>                      mu02 = (0, 0), Lambda02 = [8, 0, 0, 2]
#>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE
#> ----------------------------------------------------------------- 
#>  mu1_ep1 mu1_ep2 mu2_ep1 mu2_ep2     Go   Gray   NoGo
#>      1.0     0.5       0       0 0.0000 1.0000 0.0000
#>      2.5     1.5       0       0 0.6900 0.3100 0.0000
#>      4.0     2.5       0       0 1.0000 0.0000 0.0000
#> ----------------------------------------------------------------- 

# Example 3: Uncontrolled design, posterior probability, NIW prior
Sigma <- matrix(c(4.0, 0.8, 0.8, 1.0), 2, 2)
L0    <- matrix(c(8.0, 0.0, 0.0, 2.0), 2, 2)
pbayesdecisionprob2cont(
  nsim = 100L, prob = 'posterior', design = 'uncontrolled',
  prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart',
  GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,
  gamma1 = 0.8, gamma2 = 0.8,
  theta.TV1 = 1.5, theta.MAV1 = 0.5,
  theta.TV2 = 1.0, theta.MAV2 = 0.3,
  theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,
  n1 = 20L, n2 = NULL, m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,
  mu1 = rbind(c(1.0, 0.5), c(2.5, 1.5), c(4.0, 2.5)),
  Sigma1 = Sigma,
  mu2 = rbind(c(0.0, 0.0), c(0.0, 0.0), c(0.0, 0.0)),
  Sigma2 = Sigma,
  kappa01 = 2.0, nu01 = 5.0, mu01 = c(2.0, 1.0), Lambda01 = L0,
  kappa02 = NULL, nu02 = NULL, mu02 = c(0.0, 0.0), Lambda02 = NULL,
  r = 1.0,
  ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01e = NULL, alpha02e = NULL,
  ybar_e1 = NULL, ybar_e2 = NULL, Se1 = NULL, Se2 = NULL,
  nMC = 500L, method = 'MC',
  error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE, seed = 3L
)
#> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Two Continuous Endpoints)
#> ----------------------------------------------------------------- 
#>   Probability type : posterior
#>   Design           : uncontrolled
#>   Prior            : N-Inv-Wishart
#>   Simulations      : nsim = 100, nMC = 500
#>   Method           : MC
#>   Seed             : 3
#>   Threshold(s)     : TV1 = 1.5, MAV1 = 0.5, TV2 = 1, MAV2 = 0.3
#>   Go  threshold    : gamma1 = 0.8
#>   NoGo threshold   : gamma2 = 0.8
#>   Go  regions      : {1}
#>   NoGo regions     : {9}
#>   Sample size      : n1 = 20, n2 = NULL
#>   Prior Grp1 (NIW) : kappa01 = 2, nu01 = 5
#>                      mu01 = (2, 1), Lambda01 = [8, 0, 0, 2]
#>   Hyp. control     : mu02 = (0, 0), r = 1
#>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE
#> ----------------------------------------------------------------- 
#>  mu1_ep1 mu1_ep2     Go   Gray   NoGo
#>      1.0     0.5 0.0000 1.0000 0.0000
#>      2.5     1.5 0.6100 0.3900 0.0000
#>      4.0     2.5 1.0000 0.0000 0.0000
#> ----------------------------------------------------------------- 

# Example 4: Controlled design, predictive probability, NIW prior
Sigma <- matrix(c(4.0, 0.8, 0.8, 1.0), 2, 2)
L0    <- matrix(c(8.0, 0.0, 0.0, 2.0), 2, 2)
pbayesdecisionprob2cont(
  nsim = 100L, prob = 'predictive', design = 'controlled',
  prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart',
  GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 4L,
  gamma1 = 0.8, gamma2 = 0.8,
  theta.TV1 = NULL, theta.MAV1 = NULL,
  theta.TV2 = NULL, theta.MAV2 = NULL,
  theta.NULL1 = 0.5, theta.NULL2 = 0.3,
  n1 = 20L, n2 = 20L, m1 = 60L, m2 = 60L,
  mu1 = rbind(c(1.0, 0.5), c(2.5, 1.5), c(4.0, 2.5)),
  Sigma1 = Sigma,
  mu2 = rbind(c(0.0, 0.0), c(0.0, 0.0), c(0.0, 0.0)),
  Sigma2 = Sigma,
  kappa01 = 2.0, nu01 = 5.0, mu01 = c(2.0, 1.0), Lambda01 = L0,
  kappa02 = 2.0, nu02 = 5.0, mu02 = c(0.0, 0.0), Lambda02 = L0,
  r = NULL,
  ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01e = NULL, alpha02e = NULL,
  ybar_e1 = NULL, ybar_e2 = NULL, Se1 = NULL, Se2 = NULL,
  nMC = 500L, method = 'MC',
  error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE, seed = 4L
)
#> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Two Continuous Endpoints)
#> ----------------------------------------------------------------- 
#>   Probability type : predictive
#>   Design           : controlled
#>   Prior            : N-Inv-Wishart
#>   Simulations      : nsim = 100, nMC = 500
#>   Method           : MC
#>   Seed             : 4
#>   Threshold(s)     : NULL1 = 0.5, NULL2 = 0.3
#>   Go  threshold    : gamma1 = 0.8
#>   NoGo threshold   : gamma2 = 0.8
#>   Go  regions      : {1}
#>   NoGo regions     : {4}
#>   Sample size      : n1 = 20, n2 = 20
#>   Prior Grp1 (NIW) : kappa01 = 2, nu01 = 5
#>                      mu01 = (2, 1), Lambda01 = [8, 0, 0, 2]
#>   Prior Grp2 (NIW) : kappa02 = 2, nu02 = 5
#>                      mu02 = (0, 0), Lambda02 = [8, 0, 0, 2]
#>   Future trial     : m1 = 60, m2 = 60
#>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE
#> ----------------------------------------------------------------- 
#>  mu1_ep1 mu1_ep2 mu2_ep1 mu2_ep2     Go   Gray   NoGo
#>      1.0     0.5       0       0 0.4700 0.5300 0.0000
#>      2.5     1.5       0       0 1.0000 0.0000 0.0000
#>      4.0     2.5       0       0 1.0000 0.0000 0.0000
#> ----------------------------------------------------------------- 

# Example 5: External design (control only), posterior probability, NIW prior
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
Sigma <- matrix(c(4.0, 0.8, 0.8, 1.0), 2, 2)
L0    <- matrix(c(8.0, 0.0, 0.0, 2.0), 2, 2)
Se2   <- matrix(c(7.0, 1.2, 1.2, 1.8), 2, 2)
pbayesdecisionprob2cont(
  nsim = 100L, prob = 'posterior', design = 'external',
  prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart',
  GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,
  gamma1 = 0.8, gamma2 = 0.8,
  theta.TV1 = 1.5, theta.MAV1 = 0.5,
  theta.TV2 = 1.0, theta.MAV2 = 0.3,
  theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,
  n1 = 20L, n2 = 20L, m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,
  mu1 = rbind(c(1.0, 0.5), c(2.5, 1.5), c(4.0, 2.5)),
  Sigma1 = Sigma,
  mu2 = rbind(c(0.0, 0.0), c(0.0, 0.0), c(0.0, 0.0)),
  Sigma2 = Sigma,
  kappa01 = 2.0, nu01 = 5.0, mu01 = c(2.0, 1.0), Lambda01 = L0,
  kappa02 = 2.0, nu02 = 5.0, mu02 = c(0.0, 0.0), Lambda02 = L0,
  r = NULL,
  ne1 = NULL, ne2 = 15L, alpha01e = NULL, alpha02e = 0.5,
  ybar_e1 = NULL, ybar_e2 = c(0.2, 0.1), Se1 = NULL, Se2 = Se2,
  nMC = 500L, method = 'MC',
  error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE, seed = 5L
)
} # }

# Example 6: Controlled design, posterior probability, NIW prior, MM method
# method = 'MM' uses analytical mvtnorm::pmvt; nMC = NULL is accepted.
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
Sigma <- matrix(c(4.0, 0.8, 0.8, 1.0), 2, 2)
L0    <- matrix(c(8.0, 0.0, 0.0, 2.0), 2, 2)
pbayesdecisionprob2cont(
  nsim = 100L, prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled',
  prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart',
  GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,
  gamma1 = 0.8, gamma2 = 0.8,
  theta.TV1 = 1.5, theta.MAV1 = 0.5,
  theta.TV2 = 1.0, theta.MAV2 = 0.3,
  theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,
  n1 = 20L, n2 = 20L, m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,
  mu1 = rbind(c(1.0, 0.5), c(2.5, 1.5), c(4.0, 2.5)),
  Sigma1 = Sigma,
  mu2 = rbind(c(0.0, 0.0), c(0.0, 0.0), c(0.0, 0.0)),
  Sigma2 = Sigma,
  kappa01 = 2.0, nu01 = 5.0, mu01 = c(2.0, 1.0), Lambda01 = L0,
  kappa02 = 2.0, nu02 = 5.0, mu02 = c(0.0, 0.0), Lambda02 = L0,
  r = NULL,
  ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01e = NULL, alpha02e = NULL,
  ybar_e1 = NULL, ybar_e2 = NULL, Se1 = NULL, Se2 = NULL,
  nMC = NULL, method = 'MM',
  error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE, seed = 6L
)
} # }

# Example 7: Uncontrolled design, posterior probability, vague prior, MM method
# MM method with nMC = NULL: fully analytical, no Monte Carlo draws needed.
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
Sigma <- matrix(c(4.0, 0.8, 0.8, 1.0), 2, 2)
pbayesdecisionprob2cont(
  nsim = 100L, prob = 'posterior', design = 'uncontrolled',
  prior = 'vague',
  GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,
  gamma1 = 0.8, gamma2 = 0.8,
  theta.TV1 = 1.5, theta.MAV1 = 0.5,
  theta.TV2 = 1.0, theta.MAV2 = 0.3,
  theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,
  n1 = 20L, n2 = NULL, m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,
  mu1 = rbind(c(1.0, 0.5), c(2.5, 1.5), c(4.0, 2.5)),
  Sigma1 = Sigma,
  mu2 = rbind(c(0.0, 0.0), c(0.0, 0.0), c(0.0, 0.0)),
  Sigma2 = Sigma,
  kappa01 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, mu01 = NULL, Lambda01 = NULL,
  kappa02 = NULL, nu02 = NULL, mu02 = c(0.0, 0.0), Lambda02 = NULL,
  r = 1.0,
  ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01e = NULL, alpha02e = NULL,
  ybar_e1 = NULL, ybar_e2 = NULL, Se1 = NULL, Se2 = NULL,
  nMC = NULL, method = 'MM',
  error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE, seed = 7L
)
} # }
